Apr 10 2007 RealPlayer on the Instructional systems ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~---- Contents: RealPlayer RealPlayer on UNIX RealPlayer Mime Types RealPlayer Availability ----------------------- Realplayer

Availability ----------------------- Realplayer is the free player for the proprietary format of audio-visual files from RealNetworks, Inc (http://www.realnetworks.com). It is commonly used to play audio and video files via a WEB browser.

o play audio and video files via a WEB browser. Free copies of Realplayer for MS Windows, MacOS and several versions of UNIX are available from http://www.real.com/player/ It is installed on all Instrustional MS Windows and MacOS systems and

ll Instrustional MS Windows and MacOS systems and on the EECS Solaris SPARC UNIX systems. Realplayer can be found by running /usr/bin/realplay on a Sun Solaris SPARC UNIX system. See http://inst.eecs.berkeley.edu/~inst/iesglabs.html for the

st.eecs.berkeley.edu/~inst/iesglabs.html for the locations of these computers. RealPlayer on UNIX ------------------ For your UNIX account, you can install the MIME types and plugins needed by your browser by running the commands cd

y your browser by running the commands cd /usr/sww/pkg/RealPlayer8 ./mimeinstall.sh ./pluginstall.sh This happens automatically when you use Realplayer on Windows. EECS Instructional Support Group 378/384/386 Cory, 333
